taxonomy: Sympagis petiolaris is classified in the kingdom Animalia, phylum Arthropoda, class Insecta, order Diptera, and family Tachinidae. This classification places the species within the group of flies, specifically within the family of tachinid flies. The order Diptera is one of the largest and most diverse groups of insects, encompassing a wide range of species. Tachinid flies are parasitic, with Sympagis petiolaris likely serving as a parasite to various plant species. The family Tachinidae is composed of over 10,000 known species.
